center.name: 马纳比省
center.summary: 马纳比省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括淡眉穴䳍、Ecuadorian piculet、Velvety manakin等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Baird's flycatcher is found in the lowlands from central Manabí Province in west-central Ecuador south to Peru's Lima Department

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in western Ecuador from Manabí Province to northern Guayas Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: purpurascens has the smallest range; it is found from southwestern Colombia's Nariño Department south into northwestern Ecuador through Esmeraldas and western Pichincha provinces into northern Manabí Province. The fulvous-vented euphonia primarily inhabits the interior, edges, and clearings of tropical evergreen forest and secondary forest.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: One population is found in west-central Ecuador's Manabí and Guayas provinces

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in southwestern Ecuador from central Manabí Province south to western Guayas Province and Puná Island

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate superciliated wren is found in western Ecuador from Manabí Province south to Guayas Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: minuscula is found from the Canal Zone in Panama east into north-central Colombia to Caldas Department and south through Colombia along the Pacific slope into northwestern Ecuador as far as Manabí Province. The species inhabits humid primary forest and mature secondary forest. In elevation it ranges from sea level to on the Caribbean side of Costa Rica and to on the Pacific side. It is mostly found below elsewhere

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is native to western Ecuador; southern Manabí, Guayas, coastal and extreme western El Oro, and extreme northwestern Loja, and northwestern Peru; Tumbes and western (except coastal) Piura. It inhabits the Ecuadorian dry forests and Tumbes–Piura dry forests ecoregions

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The grey-and-white tyrannulet has a disjunct distribution It is found in southwestern Ecuador on the Isla de la Plata and Isla Puná, on the mainland in Guayas and Loja provinces, and with a single record in Manabí Province
